coverage: 100% of reachable node types across the 296-group, 2,413-node closure, including its six-object pipe structure collection.
validation: The authored six-way pipe layout, generated sleeves, SDF joint shell, dual-mesh smoothing, and structure toggle run in-browser. Convex Hull now uses the same double-precision Bullet backend as Blender: Blender's captured 28,638-point input reproduces its exact 1,507 vertices, 2,572 polygons, and 3,010 triangles. With the browser-generated input, the authored result improves to 11,225 / 8,114 versus Blender's 11,162 / 8,064 (0.56% vertex delta and exact collection bounds); joint-size is within 0.31%, and bone preview is topology-exact at 31,447 / 28,386. The remaining surface delta is upstream in the non-cap sleeve sweep positions, while hidden-structure/bone bounds differ by at most 0.0596 Blender units.
